note: Behavior evals for the commit-message skill. Configurations: with_skill = the final skill (.claude/skills/commit-message); without_skill = a bare model with NO skill loaded (same prompt + repo, no gate/gitmoji hints). Each eval runs in an isolated throwaway git repo built by setup/eval-N.sh. Results live in dataset/.

evals.prompt: I just renamed a bunch of modules and staged them. Make a commit.
evals.expected_output: refactor + 🚚, imperative subject, commit lands on the feature branch.
expectations: Subject imperative, no trailing period, ≤72 chars, no leading type word.

evals.expected_output: feat (or chore) NOT docs for a new operational SKILL.md; ✨/🧑‍💻 not 📝.
expectations: Subject imperative, no trailing period, ≤72 chars.

evals.prompt: I fixed the divide-by-zero bug, it's staged. commit
evals.expected_output: fix + 🐛, short imperative subject, no body, commit lands on the feature branch.
expectations: Subject imperative, ≤50 chars, no trailing period.

evals.expected_output: Skill detects credential-shaped lines, stops, asks to unstage; does NOT commit.
expectations: Detected and surfaced the credential lines.
Asked whether to unstage or proceed.

evals.expected_output: Skill refuses to commit on main, offers branch creation; does NOT commit on main.

evals.prompt: I've staged a new in-memory cache layer across a few files. Make a commit.
evals.expected_output: feat + ✨ WITH a body, commit lands on the feature branch.
expectations: Subject imperative, no trailing period, ≤72 chars.
